title: Values-Driven Governance

content.question: Is your next big decision aligned with your written values?
sop: Write 3-5 core values in specific, decision-usable language, not generic words like 'integrity' alone.
Before your next major decision, check it explicitly against that list.
Rate your Value Strength, 1-10 (see calculation, page 4), how clearly defined and genuinely held each value is, not just written down.
Share the values with your team or household so they're a shared standard, not a private note.
Revisit after any decision that felt hard, did the values actually guide it, or get skipped under pressure?
questions: Is your next big decision aligned with your written values?
Can you actually name your 3-5 core values right now, or would you have to think about it for the first time under pressure?
What's the last hard call you made that contradicted a value you claim to hold?
content.equation_example: Value Strength (rate 1-10: how clearly defined and consistently held your values actually are, not just written down) predicts Decision Consistency (rate 1-10: how often your choices actually match them). Value Strength 8 tends to produce Decision Consistency around 7-8; Value Strength 3 tends to produce Decision Consistency around 2-3. Higher Value Strength makes hard calls faster and more consistent; lower means every decision gets re-litigated from scratch.
content.closing_statement: Written values make hard calls faster.

caption:
Principle Nº 204: Values-Driven Governance

Root decisions in core values.

Ask yourself: Is your next big decision aligned with your written values?
